PR surface: Source +57, Tests +142, Docs +29. Total +228 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this PR implements an opt-in feature, and the changed path is source-proven with focused tests and contributor runtime proof.

target.title: feat(gateway): support Tailscale Serve service names
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an optional `gateway.tailscale.serviceName` config field for Tailscale Serve.
- Passes the configured Service name through to `tailscale serve --service=<name>` while preserving the existing device-hostname behavior when unset.
- Uses a scoped Serve cleanup command for named Services so `resetOnExit` does not reset unrelated Serve config.
- Documents the new config option in the Tailscale and gateway configuration docs.

## Linked context

Closes #88629.

Related: Tailscale's current Serve CLI exposes `--service=<name>` for Services, with `--bg` and `--yes` still available on the same command.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw had no `gateway.tailscale.serviceName` config path and always configured Serve against the node hostname with `tailscale serve --bg --yes <port>`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw contributor checkout on Linux with Tailscale CLI `1.98.3` installed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `tailscale version`
  - `tailscale serve --help > /tmp/tailscale-serve-help.txt 2>&1`
  - `rg -n -- '--service|--bg|--yes|USAGE' /tmp/tailscale-serve-help.txt`
  - focused Vitest commands listed below
- Evidence after fix: the local Tailscale help output includes `--service value`, `--bg`, and `--yes`; the new unit coverage asserts the exact generated command arguments are `tailscale serve --service=svc:openclaw --bg --yes 3000` and cleanup uses `tailscale serve --service=svc:openclaw off`.
- Observed result after fix: focused tests pass and the unchanged path still calls `enableTailscaleServe(18789)` when `serviceName` is unset.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live `tailscale serve --service=...` command against a tailnet because that would mutate real Tailscale Serve/Service state from a contributor environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proves config propagation and CLI argument generation, not a live Service advertisement in a real tailnet.
- Before evidence: current `enableTailscaleServe()` accepted only `port` and emitted `['serve', '--bg', '--yes', '<port>']`; `GatewayTailscaleConfig` had only `mode`, `resetOnExit`, and `preserveFunnel`.

## Tests and validation

- `corepack pnpm test src/infra/tailscale.test.ts src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts src/gateway/startup-auth.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed.
- `corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed.
- `corepack pnpm test src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed.
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 ...` completed on the touched TypeScript files.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, only for users who opt into `gateway.tailscale.serviceName`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Yes, the config schema accepts one new optional field. Existing config remains unchanged.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? Yes, Tailscale Serve command construction changes when the new field is set.

What is the highest-risk area? Incorrectly resetting or overwriting unrelated Tailscale Serve state. Mitigation: the existing no-service path still uses the prior `serve reset` behavior, while the named-Service path uses a scoped `tailscale serve --service=<name> off` cleanup.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review. Waiting on CI and any maintainer preference about whether this config field should also get a CLI flag in a follow-up.
target.author: charles-openclaw

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: charles-openclaw
commands.review_summary:
- Scoped `gateway.tailscale.serviceName` so it is effective only in Serve mode.
- Funnel mode now ignores the Serve-only service name for URL logging and cleanup.
- Service URL derivation now falls back to the generic enabled log when Tailscale only reports an IP or a hostname without a DNS suffix, instead of constructing an invalid `openclaw.64.0.8`-style URL.
- Added focused regression coverage for Funnel plus serviceName, Tailscale IP fallback, and missing DNS suffix behavior.

Verification:
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/gateway/server-tailscale.ts src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts src/infra/tailscale.test.ts src/config/config.gateway-tailscale-bind.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed: 4 Vitest shards, 69 tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.

@clawsweeper re-review
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger

commands.author: charles-openclaw
commands.review_summary:
- Added the Tailscale Service host prerequisite beside `gateway.tailscale.serviceName` in `docs/gateway/tailscale.md`.
- The docs now say Service hosts must be approved tagged nodes and that the Service may need admin-console approval before `tailscale serve --service=...` can publish it.

Verification:
- `corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts src/infra/tailscale.test.ts src/config/config.gateway-tailscale-bind.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed: 4 Vitest shards, 66 tests.
- Focused docs assertion confirmed the new tagged-node/Service approval text and `svc:<dns-label>` format note are present in `docs/gateway/tailscale.md`.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Additional non-mutating runtime proof:
- I ran the real `startGatewayTailscaleExposure()` helper with `tailscaleMode: "serve"`, `serviceName: "svc:openclaw"`, `resetOnExit: true`, and a temporary fake `tailscale` binary ahead of PATH so no real tailnet Serve state was changed.
- The redacted runtime log showed: `serve enabled for svc:openclaw: https://openclaw.redacted-tailnet.ts.net/ (WS via wss://openclaw.redacted-tailnet.ts.net)`.
- The fake binary captured the exact command path: `serve --service=svc:openclaw --bg --yes 18789`, `status --json`, then cleanup `serve --service=svc:openclaw off`.

I still did not run live `tailscale serve --service=...` against a real tailnet because that would mutate contributor-environment Tailscale Serve/Service state.

@clawsweeper re-review

commands.author: charles-openclaw

target.title: fix #70559: runUnsafeReindex crashes with "no such table: chunks_vec" when sqlite-vec is enabled
target.body:
## Summary

`runUnsafeReindex` crashes with `SqliteError: no such table: chunks_vec` when sqlite-vec is enabled. `resetIndex()` drops the `chunks_vec` virtual table via `dropVectorTable()`, but `this.vector.available` remains `true`. Subsequent `syncMemoryFiles`/`syncSessionFiles` check `this.vector.available === true` and call `db.prepare("DELETE FROM chunks_vec ...")`, which fails because the table no longer exists.

Fix: In `resetIndex()`, when vector is enabled and available, use row-level `DELETE FROM chunks_vec` instead of `DROP TABLE`. This preserves the table schema so subsequent prepare statements work. Row-level DELETE on `vec0` virtual tables is O(rows), same order as the existing `DELETE FROM chunks`. Falls back to original DROP behavior if DELETE fails.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `resetIndex()` calls `dropVectorTable()` which removes `chunks_vec` and sets `this.vector.dims = undefined`, but `this.vector.available` remains `true` (only set to `false` on load errors). Subsequent `syncMemoryFiles`/`syncSessionFiles` check `this.vector.available === true` and call `db.prepare("DELETE FROM chunks_vec ...")`, which fails because the table no longer exists.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The crash stems from a state inconsistency in `resetIndex()`: the vector table is dropped but `vector.available` is not updated. The fix resolves this at the source by keeping the table schema intact (DELETE instead of DROP), eliminating the inconsistency rather than catching the exception downstream.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `runUnsafeReindex` crashes with `SqliteError: no such table: chunks_vec` when sqlite-vec is enabled. `resetIndex()` drops the `chunks_vec` virtual table via `dropVectorTable()`, but `this.vector.available` remains `true`. Subsequent `syncMemoryFiles`/`syncSessionFiles` check `this.vector.available === true` and call `db.prepare("DELETE FROM chunks_vec ...")`, which fails because the table no longer exists.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node.js v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2, vitest v4.1.7
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-targeted-sync.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.vector-dedupe.test.ts`
  2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts`
  3. `git diff --check`
- **Evidence after fix:** All 44 tests pass (3 files + 1 file, 26 + 18 tests). Whitespace check passes. Only `manager-sync-ops.ts` modified; no lockfile changes. Source-level reproduction confirmed by ClawSweeper review: current `main` branch primes `vector.available`, unsafe reindex drops `chunks_vec` via `resetIndex()`, and sync setup prepares `DELETE FROM chunks_vec` before `indexFile()` can recreate the table — the crash is deterministic at the source level.
- **Observed result after fix:** No test failures, no regressions in memory index, targeted sync, vector dedupe, or search behavior. The DELETE-rows approach preserves the vector table schema, allowing subsequent prepare statements to succeed.
- **What was not tested:** Actual unsafe reindex on Windows with sqlite-vec enabled (requires Windows environment with sqlite-vec extension loaded and live agent processes holding sqlite handles).
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Fix is inside `resetIndex()` only (no downstream try/catch band-aids). Original DROP behavior retained as fallback when vector is not enabled or DELETE fails. `ensureVectorTable(dimensions)` dims-change rebuild logic is untouched. No lockfile or unrelated file changes.
- **Target test file:** `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts`

## Regression Test Plan

Existing tests covering reset + reindex + vector scenarios (`index.test.ts`, `manager-targeted-sync.test.ts`, `manager.vector-dedupe.test.ts`, `manager-search.test.ts`) all pass. The DELETE fallback path ensures unchanged behavior when vector is not enabled or the table doesn't exist. A targeted regression test for sqlite-vec-enabled unsafe reindex can be added to `manager-targeted-sync.test.ts` if needed.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
